title: 10 Herbal Essential Oils For Tendinitis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great help if you're struggling with tendinitis. This condition causes pain and inflammation in the tendons, which are the tissues that connect your muscles to your bones. Certain herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce this pain and discomfort. One of these herbs is Turmeric, or Curcuma longa, which contains a compound called curcumin. This compound has been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the tendons.  Another herb that can be beneficial for tendinitis is Eucalyptus globulus. Eucalyptus oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the tendons. It also has a cooling effect, which can help to reduce heat and inflammation in the affected area. This can make it easier to move and perform daily activities without discomfort.  Rosemary essential oil, which is extracted from the leaves of the Rosmarinus officinalis plant, can also help to relieve tendinitis. Rosemary oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce pain and swelling in the tendons. It also has a stimulating effect, which can help to improve circulation and reduce stiffness in the affected area.  Using herbal essential oils in a warm bath or applying them topically to the affected area can be a great way to relieve tendinitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs can help to reduce pain and swelling, making it easier to move and perform daily activities without discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory agent. Turmerones and ar-turmerone, other bioactive constituents, also contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce swelling and inflammation in tendons, which is a major cause of tendinitis pain. By reducing inflammation, Curcuma longa essential oils can help ease the strain on tendons and promote healing. Its antioxidant properties also help protect the tendons from further damage and promote overall health.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Curcuma longa roots and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Use a clean grinder or mortar and pestle to crush the dried Curcuma longa roots into fine powder.
Transfer the powder to a clean glass jar and mix with the carrier oil. Use 1 tablespoon of the powder for every 2 tablespoons of carrier oil.
Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This allows the essential oil to infuse into the carrier oil.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and use the infused oil for tendinitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like eucalyptol, alpha-pinene, and limonene. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in tendons. Eucalyptol helps relax muscle spasms and reduce tension, which can contribute to tendinitis. Alpha-pinene and limonene also have antioxidant properties, which can protect the tendons from damage and promote healing. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Eucalyptus globulus essential oils can help alleviate tendinitis symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 4 cups of water in a large pot.
Heat the water over medium heat and add the Eucalyptus leaves. Let it simmer for 3-4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, squeezing the leaves to get all the oil. Discard the leaves.
Let the oil cool and solidify. This may take 2-3 hours, depending on the temperature.
Once solidified, scoop the oil into a container and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops per application for tendinitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains carnosic acid, camphor, and borneol as its major bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with tendinitis. Carnosic acid, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to tissue damage and inflammation. The antioxidant properties of borneol and camphor help protect the tendons from oxidative stress, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils can help alleviate the symptoms of tendinitis and support the recovery process.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water in a pot.
Heat the water in the pot until it reaches 212°F (100°C), then reduce the heat to 140°F (60°C).
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the pot and let them steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the solids. Let the mixture sit for 24 hours.
After 24 hours, use a pipette to transfer the essential oil into a dark glass bottle. Store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium essential oils contains sesquiterpene lactones, particularly chlorogenic acid and cynaropicrin, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and pain in the affected areas, making it a good option for relieving tendinitis symptoms. The oil also contains flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that further contribute to its therapeutic benefits. The anti-inflammatory properties of Achillea millefolium essential oils help to reduce inflammation and pain in the tendons, allowing for faster recovery and relief. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Achillea millefolium essential oils can be used to alleviate tendinitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers. You can grow them in your garden or buy from a store.
Use a clean glass container and add 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil. The ratio is 1:2, flowers to oil.
Add the dried flowers to the oil and mix well. Let it sit for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place. Shake the container every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container. Discard the solids.
Bottle the essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops of the oil with a carrier oil, such as coconut oil, to massage into the affected area 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that contribute to its therapeutic properties. The saponins, specifically salvin and rosarin, have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in tendinitis. Flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin have antioxidant properties, protecting the affected tissue from further damage and promoting healing. The phenolic acids in Salvia officinalis essential oils, such as caffeic and rosmarinic acid, have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relax muscle spasms and reduce inflammation in tendinitis. These compounds work together to provide relief from the symptoms of tendinitis.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the dried leaves. Let it simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, squeezing the leaves to get as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the liquid to a distillation apparatus or a double boiler and reduce it to 1/4 cup.
Allow the liquid to cool and then decant it into a dark glass bottle. Store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected areas, making it beneficial for people with tendinitis. The warming sensation caused by the oils' gingerols helps increase blood flow to the area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness. The anti-inflammatory properties of the oils also help reduce pain and discomfort associated with tendinitis. By reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of tendinitis.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 500g of fresh Zingiber officinale roots and 5 cups of water.
Cut the roots into small pieces and place them in a large saucepan with 2 cups of water.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, then let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Use a hydrosol distiller or a still to extract the essential oil from the strained liquid, and collect 10ml of the resulting oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with tendinitis. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes and mediators, such as COX-2 and PGE2, which contribute to the development of tendinitis. The antioxidant properties of flavonoids and phenolic acids can also help protect the affected area from oxidative damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of tendinitis, such as pain and stiffness.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil)
Add 1/2 cup of dried roots to a clean glass jar and cover with 1 cup of carrier oil
Steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discard the solids
Store the Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil in a cool, dark place and use 5-7 drops per application for tendinitis relief

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and linalool. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with tendinitis. The essential oils' analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects can also help soothe and calm irritated tissues, promoting healthy healing. The anti-inflammatory properties of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils can help reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area, making it a potential remedy for tendinitis. The oil's antiseptic properties can also help prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for healing.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat until it starts to simmer.
Add the fresh leaves to the water and let it steep for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Let the liquid sit at room temperature for 2-3 days to allow the essential oil to separate and rise to the top.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains limonene, beta-pinene, and linalool as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area of tendinitis. The antispasmodic properties of limonene and linalool also ease muscle cramps and spasms associated with tendinitis. Beta-pinene's anti-inflammatory properties further contribute to reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the damaged tissues. By applying Cymbopogon citratus essential oils topically, the active constituents can directly target the affected area, providing relief from tendinitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems. Wash them with water.
Place the leaves and stems in a clean glass jar and cover them with 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Cymbopogon citratus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops in a warm bath or massage oil for tendinitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected areas, making it a potential treatment for tendinitis. The essential oils also exhibit antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles and reduce muscle spasms that are often associated with tendinitis. Additionally, the oils have analgesic properties, which can help to alleviate pain and discomfort. By reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and relieving pain, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may be a useful natural remedy for managing tendinitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil), and a clean glass jar.
Combine the Lavandula angustifolia flowers and carrier oil in the glass jar. Make sure the flowers are fully covered by the oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This will allow the oil to infuse into the carrier oil.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and keep the infused oil.
Filter the infused oil again through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any remaining particles. Your Lavandula angustifolia essential oil for tendinitis is now ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent tendinitis from forming, but it's not a guarantee. Some herbal teas, like ginger and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce muscle strain. These teas may also help soothe sore muscles and improve circulation, which can help prevent injuries and reduce the risk of tendinitis.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for tendinitis can be a helpful addition to your treatment plan, but it's essential to be cautious about daily consumption. Some herbs, like ginger and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe tendons. However, overdoing it can lead to interactions with other medications or worsen underlying conditions. Moderation is key.
faq_timeline: For herbal teas to show results in tendinitis, it can take anywhere from a few days to a couple of weeks. Some people may feel relief after a week of drinking herbal teas like turmeric or ginger. It's also possible that it takes a bit longer, up to 4-6 weeks, for the inflammation to reduce and the pain to subside.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for tendinitis can be most effective in the morning or early afternoon. This is when your body is most active and can benefit from the tea's anti-inflammatory properties. Drinking it during this time may help reduce pain and stiffness, making it easier to move and exercise.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for tendinitis. Some herbs, like ginkgo biloba and feverfew, may slow blood clotting and increase the risk of bleeding when taken with blood thinners. Ginger and turmeric can also thin the blood and affect blood pressure. This can be a concern if you're taking medications like warfarin or aspirin.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas for tendinitis. Some herbs, like willow bark, contain salicylates similar to aspirin, which may affect the baby's development. Ginger and peppermint teas are usually safe, but it's essential to check the ingredients and potential interactions. Stick to well-known, gentle herbs to avoid any risks.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs can pass into breast milk, which might affect your baby. If you're considering herbal teas for tendinitis, look for ones that are specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding. These teas are usually made from gentle herbs, but always check the ingredients to be sure.
title_2: Essential Oils to Help Athletes with Tendinitis: 10 Options
